It mislabels a Wi‑Fi extension as “WiFi 8”, conflating it with the forthcoming IEEE 802.11bn standard, suggesting the author either overlooked current Wi‑Fi nomenclature or used AI help. The draft also promises no app changes, though existing code assumes 32‑bit IPv4 addresses, making compatibility unrealistic.

title: Rewriting Every Syscall in a Linux Binary at Load Time
content: This post discusses a method for reconstructing every system call used by a Linux binary at load time. The discussion covers implementation steps, potential attack vectors, and implications for reverse engineering and binary hardening.

title: 忘掉小鹏汽车
content: The article recounts Xpeng’s transformation from a pure‑electric niche brand to a broader technology company. After costly missteps in 2022, CEO He Xiaopeng shifted strategy to include plug‑in hybrids, enabling faster global expansion. Product launches accelerated, and the company built a unified AI platform—VLA, Turing AI chips, and Robotaxi tech—that it now sells to partners like VW. Renamed Xpeng Group in 2026, the firm aims to profit from AI‑driven hardware, while facing intense competition and market skepticism.

title: 不缺钱的梁文锋，为什么要融资？
content: DeepSeek, led by Liang Wenfeng, has long avoided external funding, focusing on low‑cost, high‑impact research. With several key researchers leaving for larger firms and product development stalled, the company’s competitive edge has weakened. Seeking $300 million at a $10 billion valuation, DeepSeek aims to stabilize its equity, accelerate V4 development—including full Huawei Ascend 950 integration—and shift from a pure research lab to a commercial, product‑centric model. This funding marks a strategic pivot to sustain talent and infrastructure amid an accelerating AI market.

title: 《经济学人》封面文章丨美国终于意识到人工智能的致命威力 | 原文

It criticizes the prior laissez‑faire regulatory approach, arguing that concentrating control in a few prominent figures—such as Solow, Demis Hassabis, Elon Musk, Mark Zuckerberg, and Sam Altman—poses strategic risks. The piece notes that the Trump administration relied on unrestricted corporate competition to stay ahead in the AI race, but this stance has become politically untenable and strategically flawed given the rapid advances in AI technology.

所有人都看得见的趋势、一致预判的业绩增速、人人皆知的利好，早早被股价充分定价。贝塔的钱，是随波逐流的大势钱，没有额外超额。
而阿尔法，核心本质就是预期差。
举个通…
source: 热帖 - 雪球 - 韭菲特漂流记

title: 7 家平台涉「幽灵外卖」被罚没近 36 亿元：拼多多 15.22 亿、美团 7.46 亿、阿里系 6.3 亿
content: In April 2024, the State Administration for Market Regulation fined seven e‑commerce platforms—Pinduoduo, Meituan, JD.com, Taobao Flash Sale, Douyin, Taobao, and Tmall—for “ghost delivery” violations. The administration revoked licenses, suspended new cake vendor listings for 3–9 months, and imposed a collective fine of 3.597 billion yuan, with 19.6874 million yuan levied on company leaders and food‑safety directors. The platforms were found to have weak permit checks, failed to verify qualifications, and neglected to regulate order‑transfer practices, leading to the removal of ghost shops and cessation of such collaborations.

title: MacBook Neo 全球销售火爆，多个市场发货排期已至 5 月
content: Apple's MacBook Neo is selling out worldwide; April orders are full and shipping is now scheduled for May. Availability is limited even in physical stores, with some outlets holding only a few colors and restocking mid‑May, while third‑party delivery is delayed by at least a week. Demand exceeded expectations, prompting Apple to raise its 2026 shipment target from 5–6 million to 10 million units. The Neo has set a record for the highest number of first‑time Mac buyers in a single week, marking a key step in expanding the user base.

title: 杭州六小龙之一群核科技港股上市，开盘大涨 170%
content: Qunhe Technology, one of Hangzhou’s “Six Little Dragons,” listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange today with a share price of HK$7.62. The stock closed at HK$20.62, up more than 170%. The IPO will offer approximately 161 million shares, raising up to HK$1.227 billion. Qunhe is a leading provider of space‑intelligence services, operating the global 3D interior platform 3D X and the overseas version Coohom, serving over 200 countries. Other Hangzhou startups in the group include Shendu Quesuo, Yushutong, Game Science, Yunshen Chu, and Qiangna Tech.

title: OpenAI 据悉将斥资超 200 亿美元购买 Cerebras 芯片并获得股份
content: OpenAI has agreed to spend more than $20 billion over three years to use Cerebras chips in its servers, potentially earning equity in the chip startup. In January, OpenAI also committed up to 750 MW of compute from Cerebras, worth over $10 billion. The deal may add up to 10 % equity in Cerebras, which plans an IPO in Q2 and a $3 billion raise next month. Cerebras, founded in 2015 and backed by OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, is known for wafer‑scale engine chips.

title: OpenAI 推出药物研发 AI 模型 GPT-Rosalind 挑战谷歌
content: OpenAI released an early version of GPT‑Rosalind, an AI model aimed at accelerating drug discovery. The specialized model targets life‑science research and will be available to select corporate clients on a research preview basis, with initial users including Amgen, Moderna, and the Allen Institute. The launch joins efforts from OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google to apply artificial intelligence to scientific and medical advancements.

title: OpenAI ChatGPT 全球周活跃用户逼近 10 亿，女性占比超 50%
content: OpenAI reports that ChatGPT’s weekly active users are near 1 billion, with women making up over half of this base—about 500 million women use the service regularly. The company’s compute capacity has grown from 0.2 peta‑flops in 2023 to roughly 1.9 peta‑flops in 2025, tripling annually. OpenAI has secured more than 30 peta‑flops of power in agreements with partners such as NVIDIA, targeting 30 peta‑flops by 2030.

content: The article reports on the controversy over the Gulf of Hormuz free navigation. Iran’s navy condemns U.S. maritime blockade as “piracy,” while U.S. forces maintain full surveillance of Iranian ports and say the blockade will continue if needed. Iran’s foreign ministry says only non‑military vessels may pass the strait and that enriched uranium cannot leave the country. Conflicting statements from Washington and Tehran, combined with the U.S. military’s return of ships, have sparked market shocks and further tensions.

title: 黑猩猩内战的启示：分裂与暴力如何产生的？ | 原文
content: A long‑term study of a chimpanzee group in Uganda’s Kibale National Park shows that internal conflict arises from relationship breakdowns rather than deep group divisions. In 2015, researchers observed a sustained six‑week avoidance behavior when Western and Central subgroups met: one group fled while the other pursued. This pattern suggests that personal grievances and social fractures drive violence within chimp communities, mirroring how human conflicts may stem from interpersonal ruptures instead of entrenched ideology.

title: Building an emoji list generator with the GitHub Copilot CLI
content: The blog post describes how the GitHub team created an emoji‑list generator during a live build session. Using the GitHub Copilot CLI, the author generated a terminal tool that lets users paste or type bullet points, automatically replaces them with relevant emojis via the Copilot SDK, and copies the result to the clipboard. Technologies used include OpenTUI for the terminal UI and clipboardy for clipboard access. The project is free, open source, and built with plan, autopilot, and multi‑model workflows.

title: 一文看懂光通信，赚钱必看！
content: The article explains that light‑communication stocks are now being priced primarily on upstream supplies. It traces the current hype from optical modules, to the chips that feed them, to the scarce raw materials like indium phosphide and lithium niobate. Module makers lack enough chip supply, so demand for chip suppliers surges, pushing their valuations higher. Thus, investors should focus on materials suppliers rather than just module or chip companies, as the next growth will likely come from those raw‑material producers.
source: 热帖 - 雪球 - 退学博士炒股票

title: 成功被 Anthropic 毕业.
content: In April 2026, a user graduated from Anthropic and survived for 12 days. They paid via the company’s Mercury account using a dedicated virtual card, accessed the internet through a chained proxy with fixed Singapore/USA IPs at an IDC, and logged into Claude services from a single machine. They subscribed to a $20 Pro plan, mainly using Singapore IPs, switching briefly to a US IP due to instability, and later received an email. They attempted re-registration with a Singapore IP, which now requires phone verification, and further support for OpenAI is available.

title: Congrats to the Notion MCP Challenge Winners!
content: The Notion MCP Challenge announced its winners: first place, NoteRunway, an AI‑powered workspace manager that uses a hybrid API and MCP approach to keep Notion organized; runner‑ups, DevNotion, which turns weekly GitHub activity into a blog post and Notion planner, and Relay, an incident‑management assistant that documents incidents in real time via MCP. Winners receive $500, DEV++ subscriptions, and a badge; other participants earn a completion badge. The announcement also promotes upcoming DEV Weekend Earth Day and OpenClaw challenges with prize pools.

title: Bringing more transparency to GitHub’s status page
content: GitHub is enhancing its status page to improve transparency and accuracy. It introduces a “Degraded Performance” tier, a three‑level incident classification, and publishes per‑service uptime for the last 90 days. Uptime calculations use severity weights: Major Outage 100%, Partial Outage 30%, Degraded Performance 0%. A new “Copilot AI Model Providers” component will report model‑provider outages separately from the broader Copilot service. These changes aim to give users clearer, more actionable service health information.
